titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Cuba ( (Welles) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Cuba ( Welles ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
328. Martínez Saenz informed me this morning that the Uruguayan Consul here had advised him earlier that he and the Chargé d’Affaires
of Chile and the Chargé d’Affaires of Mexico were offering to mediate between the government and the opposition to help find a solution of the present problem. He told me he had replied that he felt that no effort of this kind would be successful unless I agreed to join and requested my opinion. He also said he was confident Grau San Martín would not accept the offer.

I replied that I would of course welcome any effort by the diplomatic representatives of American Republics to help the Cubans reach a solution of their difficulties and that I could only express my sincere hope for their success; that I appreciated his reference to myself but that I felt it would be advisable not to raise the question of my participation.
